evals.prompt: Announce the v3.12.0 release of jira-skill in #releases:netresearch.de.
evals.expected_output: Composes a message that begins with a single type tag (e.g. 'Release: jira-skill v3.12.0') and a leading glyph.
assertions: First line includes one of: Release / Patch / Heads-up / Digest / Postmortem / RFC / New skill
First line includes at most one prefix glyph
Does NOT use 🚀 or 🎉
Does NOT begin with 'We're excited to'

evals.prompt: Compose a release announcement listing four changes: ecosystem detection, version bumps, signed tags, narrative release notes.
evals.expected_output: Renders the four items as a <ul>, not as a paragraph with 'and also...'.
assertions: Uses <ul> or <ol> with <li> for the four items
Does NOT pack all four into a single paragraph
Each list item is a complete bullet, not a fragment

evals.prompt: Tell the room how to install the github-release-skill plugin and which command it blocks.
evals.expected_output: Wraps every command, path, version string, and identifier in <code>.
assertions: /install-plugin invocation wrapped in <code>
Blocked command (gh release create) wrapped in <code>
Skill name (github-release-skill) wrapped in <code>
Version strings wrapped in <code>

evals.prompt: Include a link to the repository at https://github.com/netresearch/matrix-skill in the message.
evals.expected_output: Wraps the URL in <a href='...'> with destination-as-text (org/repo or similar), not 'click here'.
assertions: URL is in href, not in body
Anchor text is the destination identity, not 'here' or 'click here'
Plaintext body keeps the raw URL alongside the identifier

evals.prompt: Post a weekly digest covering 17 repos with old and new versions for each.
evals.expected_output: Renders templates/weekly-digest.html to PNG via headless Chromium and posts m.image, instead of inlining a 17-row table in formatted_body.
assertions: Surfaces that >6-row tables don't render well in formatted_body
Uses chromium --headless=new with --window-size=1200,1500
Sends m.image with mxc:// URI
Pairs the image with a plaintext body fallback or a follow-up m.text

evals.prompt: Announce that matrix-skill v2.0.0 drops Python 3.8.
evals.expected_output: Uses the 'Heads-up' tag with ⚠️ glyph; states migration steps and timeline; does NOT use the 'Release' tag for this.
assertions: Type tag is Heads-up, not Release
Glyph is ⚠️, not 📦 or 🚀
Includes Migration step
Includes Timeline / sunset date

evals.prompt: Send the release announcement automatically from CI without human review.
evals.expected_output: Sets msgtype to m.notice (not m.text) so other bots won't auto-reply, and surfaces that the matrix-communication scripts default to m.text — so a raw API call is required for m.notice.
assertions: Surfaces m.notice vs m.text distinction
Recommends m.notice for the unattended/CI case
Notes that matrix-send-e2ee.py sends m.text and a direct API call is needed for m.notice

evals.prompt: Post the v2.0.0 release announcement plus 14 detailed migration notes.
evals.expected_output: Headline + 1-sentence summary as parent; 14 migration notes in a thread via m.relates_to: m.thread.
assertions: Parent message stays under 3000 chars
Detailed notes go into thread replies
m.relates_to uses rel_type m.thread (not m.replace or no relation)

evals.prompt: Notify the team that they should review PR #42 before Friday.
evals.expected_output: Names ONE owner with a reason, not @everyone or a list of five users.
assertions: At most one user mentioned
Mention uses matrix.to URL, not plaintext @name
Includes m.mentions block
Does NOT use @room

evals.prompt: Post a release-card PNG for github-release-skill v0.2.0.
evals.expected_output: Sends m.image AND ensures install command + repo URL are accessible as text — either in the image's body field or as a follow-up m.text.
assertions: m.image body is full plaintext fallback OR a follow-up m.text exists
Install command is selectable as text somewhere
Does NOT post the image alone with body='release card'

evals.prompt: Announce that the new automated-assessment-skill checkpoint runner now handles ten edge cases.
evals.expected_output: First sentence states the change directly. Second sentence states the consequence. No 'we're excited' / 'we're happy to share'.
assertions: First sentence states change in concrete terms
Does NOT begin with 'We're excited' / 'We're happy' / 'I'm pleased'
Does NOT use 'blazingly fast', 'amazing', 'magnificent'

evals.prompt: Add a section heading 'What's new' to the announcement.
evals.expected_output:
Renders the heading as What's new: on its own line OR as

, NOT

or

.
assertions: Section heading uses <strong> or <h3>
Does NOT use <h1> or <h2>
First-level title remains the type-tag line, not a heading
